About · Vent Atelier

About Vent Atelier

We make small instruments that translate big phenomena. AURORA is the first.

Vent Atelier is a small studio in Hayward, California. Three engineers, one optics specialist, one industrial designer. We don't have a marketing department. We do have a calibration room that takes about an hour to warm up.

Our shared bias is toward instruments that are honest about their inputs. Most "smart" devices smear out the data they consume - they round, they interpolate, they make the world feel smoother than it is. We prefer devices that show you the actual texture of the thing they're tracking, even when that texture is jagged. The aurora is jagged. AURORA is jagged back.

The studio is named after hydrothermal vents on the deep ocean floor - the places where chemoautotrophic life thrives without sunlight, getting its energy from sulfur compounds rising out of the seafloor. The reference is partly because the founder grew up near one such vent community in the Pacific. It's mostly because we like the idea of instruments that quietly metabolize the world around them, without asking the user to feed them anything.

We sell directly. No distributors, no Amazon listing. If you want one, pre-order from us. If you want to know how it works, the science and mission pages cover that.

Imagery on this site comes from NASA's public-domain archives. Full attribution lives at CITATIONS.

Imagery courtesy NASA, public domain. Full citations.
